sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Real Betis are struggling, with no wins in their last 4 matches and a consistent trend of conceding goals (no clean sheets in 10 games). Valencia, despite mixed results, showed attacking intent with a 3-0 win recently and a narrow loss, indicating some resilience.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: The last two encounters between these teams were high-scoring, with Valencia winning 4-2 at home recently and Real Betis securing a 2-1 victory earlier. Matches tend to produce over 2.5 goals, with Real Betis often scoring first.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: Isco (Real Betis) with a 7.5 average rating and Hugo Duro (Valencia) with 11 goals this season are pivotal. Betis' in-form players like Antony and Cucho Hernández could exploit Valencia's defensive vulnerabilities.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Real Betis rank higher in possession (6th) and corners per game (4th), while Valencia struggle defensively, conceding more goals (53 vs. Betis' 49). Betis' ability to create big chances (82) could be decisive at home.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Real Betis are likely to dominate possession and press in the final third (4th in pressing rank), while Valencia might rely on counter-attacks with Hugo Duro leading the line. Defensive frailties on both sides suggest a goal-heavy game.
